retrieved_contexts:
TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What causes an 1152 error during extraction of Modeler self extracting executable? 

CAUSE
When an IBM SPSS compressed image is launched, files compressed within it are extracted to the temporary location assigned to the signed in user i.e. /Documents and settings/... or /users.
The possibility of these files downloaded improperly exists. You can use a tool such
as MD5 to verify the file content or simply attempt downloading a second time and test.

Once that you have confirmed that Modeler Client is operational, repeat the steps to extract the .msi file for Modeler Client Fix Pack 1. Please note that it is highly recommended to delete the contents of the temporary folder as the Fix Pack and Client installations use the same file names. This is to prevent the accidental copying of the Client installation instead of the Fix Pack install.

* Run the installer executable (SPSS_Modeler_141_win64.exe) as normal. When it finishes extracting and decompressing the .msi file and the end-user is required to click 'Continue', minimise this window. 

* Switch to the %temp% folder and locate the folder with the 'ModelerClient64.msi' file. Copy ALL contents within this folder and paste it to a local folder (e.g. C:\Temp\Modeler\ClientInstall). 

* Once copied, switch back to the installation wizard and hit 'Cancel'.

C:\>SPSS_Statistics_24_win64.exe /b"C:\MSItest" 


3. Notice that running this command will launch the InstallShield Wizard. Let this run until the .msi has been extracted. You will see "Extracting: IBM SPSS Statistics 24.msi" message from the installer. You do not need to proceed with the full installation. 

Using Installation Media (CD/DVD)
1. Insert the disk.
2. IF prompted to install the software, select 'Cancel'.
3. Browse to the media location. 
4. Open the 'SPSSStatistics' folder.

* Once copied, switch back to the installation wizard and hit 'Cancel'. 

Step 2: Use the command prompt to execute the 'MsiExec' command: 

* Open a command prompt window as an Administrator and run the following command: (substitute <path> with the location of your .msi file) 

MsiExec.exe /i "<path>\ModelerClient.msi" /qn /L*v logfile.txt INSTALLDIR="C:\Program Files\IBM\SPSS\Modeler\14" LICENSETYPE="Network" LSHOST="mylicserver"
per_question.response: The 1152 error during the extraction of the Modeler self-extracting executable can occur if the files within the IBM SPSS compressed image were downloaded improperly. It is recommended to verify the file content using a tool like MD5 or to attempt downloading the file again to resolve this issue.

WSRdbManagedC > preTestConnection Entry WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl@51a507f2 query: SELECT 1 FROM DUAL WSRdbManagedC 3 Exception java.sql.SQLException: ORA-02396: exceeded maximum idle time, please connect again DSRA0010E: SQL State = 61000, Error Code = 2,396 at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIoer.processError(T4CTTIoer.java:440) at

retrieved_contexts:
RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Configure the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v7r0/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.websphere.nd.multiplatform.doc/info/ae/ae/udat_conpoolset.html] so as to eliminate occurrences of StaleConnectionException :

ENVIRONMENT
The exception com.ibm.websphere.ce.cm.StaleConnectionException is specific to WebSphere Application Server environments. But a similar issue can be experienced with Weblogic, see: Rule Execution Server on WebLogic unable to load ruleset after period of inactivity [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21615801]

If the J2CA0036E and DSRA0023E are logged in the application server's SystemOut.log, then check the JDBC Provider configuration to see if it uses WebSphere variables for its classpath setting. From the Administrative Console select Resources > JDBC Provider > DB_Provider_Used_By_DataSource, and inspect the classpath. For this example, one would check to see if the JDBC Provider configuration uses the Application Server's DB2_INSTALL_ROOT and DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH variable.

per_question.response: To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, configure the data source connection pool to eliminate occurrences of this exception. Additionally, ensure that the JDBC Provider configuration uses the Application Server's DB2_INSTALL_ROOT and DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH variables for its classpath setting, as indicated in the context.
per_question.reference:
To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, you need to set the "Validate on Reserve" property to true for the connection pool associated with the data source in WebSphere Application Server. This setting will ensure that the connections are validated before being returned to the application to prevent stale connections. 

Additionally, you may need to adjust the maximum idle time setting in the Oracle database to prevent the ORA-02396 error. This can be done by modifying the SQLNET.ORA file on the Oracle server to increase the value of the SQLNET.EXPIRE_TIME parameter. 

By setting "Validate on Reserve" to true in the connection pool and adjusting the maximum idle time in the Oracle database, you should be able to resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server.

To resolve a WSVR0605W hung thread issue in the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter, you need to tune the thread pool size for the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter. By default, the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter thread pool is created with a minimum of 2 threads and a maximum of 20 threads. The Growable option is set to true, allowing the number of threads to go beyond the maximum if needed. To prevent blocked threads, you may need to limit and tune the maximum thread pool size. Individual performance tuning in a pre-production test environment is recommended to avoid performance problems on a production server.

You can access the configuration for work managers in the administrative console under Resources > Asynchronous beans > Work managers > WMQJCAResourceAdapter. It is important to configure work managers according to your specific requirements and workload to prevent hung thread issues.

TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I export and import private keys between the same or different Hardware Security Module (HSM) enabled IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance? 

ANSWER
HSM-enabled DataPower appliances support the export of private keys using the crypto-export command. For key export to work, various conditions must be met:

As for the certificate configuration on DataPower, the appliance will reference the actual key and certificate files stored in the cert: directory by file name. Navigation in the WebGUI to the SSL Proxy Profile -> Crypto Profile -> Crypto Key and Certificate objects will show the current files referenced by the configuration.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3]

Once all of this has been done, then private keys may move from system to system with crypto-export and crypto-import. 

Note that the non-HSM appliance can export keys immediately at keygen time, but never at a later time. 

For additional information refer to the online Info Center [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v3r8m2/index.jsp?topic=%2Fxi50%2Fwelcome.htm] and search for "PIN entry device".
. If the goal is to move exported keys from one appliance to another one, then all of the steps in this section must be followed :-

ANSWER
To add the Data Integration Module: 

 * The appliance must be a DataPower Service Gateway XG45 running firmware 4.0.2.0 or later. 
 * You purchased the Data Integration Module and received a letter that contains the Controlled Access Key to download the firmware image.
per_question.response: To export a private key from a DataPower Gateway appliance, you can use the crypto-export command, ensuring that all necessary conditions are met for the export process. Once the conditions are satisfied, you can move private keys between systems using the crypto-export and crypto-import commands.
per_question.reference:
To export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance, you can use the `crypto-export` command. However, to successfully export a private key, the following conditions must be met:

1. Ensure that the HSMs on both the exporting and importing machines are initialized and in the same key sharing domain.
2. The private key you want to export must be marked as exportable at the time of key generation.
3. The HSMs on both machines must share internal key-wrapping keys. These keys encrypt the exported private keys and decrypt imported private keys.
4. If you are moving keys from one appliance to another, synchronize the key-wrapping keys using the `hsm-clone-kwk` command on both appliances.

Follow these steps to successfully export a private key from a DataPower Gateway Appliance using the `crypto-export` command.

per_question.user_input: When should I use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers? MQ version 7.0 introduced a new feature, conversation sharing. Are there any special values on DataPower when configuring conversation sharing?
retrieved_contexts:
The default setting of sharing conversations (SHARECNV) value is zero in DataPower mq-qm object. The same default value for MQ SVRCONN channel is 10. The sharing conversations value is 
negotiated between MQ server and DataPower and the lower value takes effect. However, in current versions of the DataPower firmware, the sharing conversations setting of 1 is treated as 0 when negotiating with MQ server.

For DataPower firmware releases 7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x and earlier, one should follow case #1 and use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0. If you configure these firmware versions with case #2 or case #3, DataPower device may reload or hang due to connection instability and generate many MQ errors with Reason Code - 2009 and 2059.

Set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower as shown in the following picture and a value of 1 on IBM WebSphere MQ SVRCONN setting.

There are three use cases to configure Sharing Conversations in DataPower mq-qm object:

   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.

per_question.reference: The file db2jcc.jar is missing after an upgrade to BPM V8.5.7 CF2016.12 because IBM BPM does not transform complex IBM BPM types (such as TWObjects) to standard JDBC types that the database can process. The FSH_B is disabled, thus MPGW_B cannot take traffic. However, if I modify the config for MPGW_B (i.e. just change a Stylesheet parameter) the traffic goes to MPGW_B somehow. How is this happening? FSH_B is still disabled.
retrieved_contexts: The output above shows that three default gateways were configured on eth0, eth1, and mgt0 which causes three "default" route to be generated (0.0.0.0/0). If the destination address of your service is 166.84.23.4, the device will randomly choose any one of the default routes as no specific route is configured. If, for example, the gateway picked is 10.238.77.1 and the gateway does not know how to route to the needed backend server ip address, this will cause unexpected connection failures

If you want your network traffic to follow a specific route, set a static route and NOT rely on the default gateway. As a general rule, the management interface should not have the default gateway set, as you generally want to restrict the connections that can be made through that interface. 

A general recommendation is to set specific static routes and only ONE default gateway that will catch all traffic not being directed by the static routes.

If inbound and outbound SMTP traffic is separate and the servers are in two different domains, this is very problematic.

If there is cross-certification set up between Domain A and Domain B for user access, it is possible that this cross-certification is allowing traffic to flow from Domain A to Domain B, even if the FSH_B is disabled in MPGW_B.

per_question.response: Yes, the Java.io.NotSerializableException occurs when an object that does not implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface is placed in a session. This often results in repeated entries in the server log, such as the error message WTRN0047E, indicating that the object causing the issue is likely an attribute of the session object or referenced by it.
per_question.reference:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.

per_question.user_input: Why does my JVMSERVER remain in a "BEING DISABLED"  state? When I try to disable a JVMSERVER it becomes hung and remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state. This also occurs when I shut down the CICS region. The CICS region ultimately has to be cancelled.
retrieved_contexts:
SYMPTOM
DFHSJ0215 [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v5r1/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/DFHSJ/DFHSJ0215.html] The JVM Server jvmserver failed to initialize the OSGi framework. The JVM will be terminated. 

DFHSJ0914 [http://pic.dhe.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v5r1/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.messages.doc/DFHSJ/DFHSJ0914E.html] JVMSERVER jvmserver is DISABLED because the Language Environment Enclave was not created.


CAUSE
zFS files not propagated to target environment(s)

If using CICS TS 4.2 with JVM servers, enter CEMT SET JVMSERVER(jvmserver) DISABLED [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.systemprogramming.doc/commands/dfha8_setjvmserver.html] then ENABLED to disable and enable the JVMSERVER [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/cicsts/v4r2/topic/com.ibm.cics.ts.resourcedefinition.doc/resources/jvmserver/dfha4_overview.html] resource that the Java program uses.
Please see your application server documentation for how to disable this capability.

long as the thread is detected as hung, using the
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track property.
   If this property is unset, the default value is set to be
   identical to the value of
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.  If the property is
   set to "true", the thread monitor sets those properties to the
   maximum integer value, so a thread hung for a long period of
   time would cause javacores to be thrown basically
   indefinitely.

There are 3 options to correct this:

1) Try the classification "add attribute" function and consider increasing JVM memory if needed. This is the officially supported way to handle your use case. Note, delete the attribute before trying to add it again.

2) Delete all of your authorized CIs and then promote again. Do not use 
the CI Space Loader tool to load new attributes and you will not have 
this issue again.
per_question.response: Your JVMSERVER remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state because the Language Environment Enclave was not created, which leads to the server being disabled as indicated by the DFHSJ0914 message. Additionally, if zFS files are not propagated to the target environment, it can cause issues with the initialization of the OSGi framework, resulting in the JVM being terminated and potentially leading to hung states during disablement.
per_question.reference:
Based on the provided context, the issue with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state could be due to the zFS files not being properly propagated to the target environments after applying preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS. When the updated zFS files are not rolled out to the target environments, it can impact the functioning of the JVM Servers.

To resolve this issue, ensure that the updated zFS files are properly propagated to all target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS. This should allow the JVMSERVER to be disabled successfully without getting stuck in a "BEING DISABLED" state.

per_question.user_input: Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method? Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests?
retrieved_contexts:
ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Datapower service (i.e MPGW, WSP, etc) is configured to connect
   to backend server through an HTTP Proxy server (as configured
   in the Proxy Policy of the user agent) using the HTTP CONNECT
   method.
   However, if the HTTP CONNECT request to the proxy server,
   receives a 5xx response, Datapower might issue the following SSL
   error:
   20160711T163325.760Z [0x8120002f][ssl][error]
   ssl-client(PMYClientProfile): tid(11573565)[10.x.x.x]

Users configuring DataPower may need a way to implement this CONNECT method for SSL tunneling to a remote Proxy Server.




RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Non-SSL Proxy requests will work using either:

a) an XML Firewall when configuring in the HTTP Options tab, where the user can configure the Proxy Host and Port

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

SSL Proxy requests will work using the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

The XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling.

For DataPower versions 6.0.0 and later:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_NULL_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA256

For DataPower versions prior to 6.0.0:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
AES_SHA_US

(b). In WebSphere MQ channels select the same TLS protocol as DataPower uses from the channel CipherSpec.

When DataPower mq-qm object is using an SSL Proxy Profile:

Forward Proxy XMLFW MPGW CONNECT TUNNEL TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 How to configure an IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance Service to proxy an SSL request to a remote proxy server using the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling? 

CAUSE
Many common forward proxy servers expect requests in different formats depending on if they are proxying non-SSL or SSL requests.

For example, a non-SSL request to be proxied may come in the form of GET hostname:port/URI.
per_question.response: Yes, the IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway configured in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy. However, the XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling.
per_question.reference: Yes, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance does support the HTTP CONNECT method. Specifically, for SSL proxy requests, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway and configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

per_question.user_input:
Datacap on Citrix  Hi All,

Can we operate Datacap thin clients on citrix?
retrieved_contexts: Some customers successfully operate Datacap thick clients in remote sites using Citrix or other remote access technology. IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If you deploy Datacap clients on Citrix and encounter issues, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.

WAN LAN architecture deployment TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What are the best practices for deploying Datacap servers and clients on a wide area network (WAN)? 

ANSWER
Remote users that access Datacap over a WAN can use Taskmaster Web-based "thin clients", or FastDoc Capture operating in offline mode. Datacap thick clients (DotScan, DotEdit) and utilities (NENU, Fingerprint Maintenance Tool) require LAN communication speeds and low latency for responsive performance.

1. On client system open a Command Prompt window 
 2. Type
    cd c:\Datacap\Taskmaster 
 3. Then type
    dcskey.exe -i 
 4. If you are still unable to connect then Remove the client system from the domain 
 5. On the client delete the local folder of the domain user 
 6. Reconnect the client to the domain 
 7. Log into client with domain user 
 8. Repeat steps 1 through 4 and connect with DotEdit and DotScan

Q. I read in the forum that the Admin client and the Designer client are not supported on Citrix. Is that correct? 
A. Correct, neither the Domino Administrator client nor the Domino Designer clients are supported on Citrix. 



ORIGINAL PUBLICATION DATE
 2011/7/1

. That should be able to handle that properly. There's been some discussion of apparently some shortcomings when you either move replicas or when you move the cache on the client. It's not clear to me what all the issues are there. Without specific cases and without it a reproducible scenario, it's hard for me to answer that specifically.
per_question.reference: Based on the provided context, Datacap thin clients can be operated on Citrix. However, it is mentioned that IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If any issues are encountered while deploying Datacap clients on Citrix, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.
